reasoning: Cluster 1 (Infrastructure/Oracle/L2: LINK, OP, ARB, ENS, GRT) presents a highly conflicted signal environment. Strong short-term momentum (+18.23% 7-day, +19.73% 14-day) suggests a sharp relief rally, likely driven by broader crypto sentiment or L2 narrative rotation. However, the overarching bear regime (strong downtrend), poor 30-day return (-1.09%), deep max drawdown (-21.2%), and negative Sharpe (-0.13) all argue against sustained continuation. The TVL data is sparse for this cluster (infrastructure/governance tokens have limited protocol revenue capture), weakening fundamental support. The positive 14-day momentum is annualized to ~514%, which is clearly unsustainable — mean reversion risk is high. Discounting heavily for bear regime persistence and momentum decay, a modest positive annualized return of ~42% is projected for the next 14-day window, reflecting a tapering but not fully reversed momentum impulse. Confidence is low (0.31) given the severe tension between short-term momentum and medium-term bear trend, high volatility (72.7% annualized), and absence of fundamental TVL anchors for most tokens in the cluster.
